competitive_position: Coupa is the live peer threat, priced at €475K SaaS (vs Zycus €448,680), both within buyer's stated 400-500K range. Zycus judged stronger on S2C and AI per Jun 2026 POC feedback ('AI features well received and considered differentiators; S2C judged stronger than competitors'), but Coupa remains a pricing benchmark and buyer is building 'side-2-side comparison' (21 May Next_Step). Orchestration overlay (Tronkeon from unnamed vendor) emerged 24 Jun as structural fallback risk if IT blocks full-suite implementation — attractive to IT (no SAP effort) but inferior for procurement. Walmedien / Allocation Network is the aging P2P incumbent being replaced. Do-nothing is a live risk if IT refuses resources and CFO cannot override. Single strongest CURRENT threat: IT resource block (do-nothing), not a vendor — if IT refuses resources, deal is deferred to 2028+ regardless of Zycus fit or Coupa pricing.

champion.source: 1 Jul 2026 call (Pölki); 16 Mar 2026 call (Eppich): 'expressed her preference for Zycus'; 24 Jun 2026 call: 'procurement team attempting to build internal pressure: daily discussions with boss, engaging controlling, seeking more internal partners to push project'
champion.summary: Engelbert Pölki and Karin Eppich are strong champions on the procurement side, actively building internal pressure and preferring Zycus. However, they cannot override CIO/IT resistance without CFO intervention. Champion strength is strong on procurement, but insufficient authority to close.
champion.champion: Engelbert Pölki (Manager Strategic Projects IT & Supply Chain), Karin Eppich (Purchasing processes and systems)

items.quote: CFO Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner is named in CRM MEDDPICC as Economic Buyer, but has not appeared on any recorded call or committed budget/resources. Buyer requested management meeting 25 Jun for 1 Jul; if CFO does not attend or cannot override IT, deal is structurally stalled.

CIO/IT estimates 170 man-days SAP analysis; Zycus proposed AI iSaaS to reduce to ~30 days, but buyer stated 24 Jun 'IT will say no resources available regardless of reduced effort.' Zycus has not yet presented AI iSaaS proposal directly to CIO. Without CIO buy-in, IT will block implementation.

items.area: Quantified value case (ROI, cycle time reduction, cost savings)
items.quote: No quantified ROI, cycle time reduction, or cost savings has been articulated on any call. Buyer stated 9 Jun 'we need a strong business case to compete for budget.' Quantified value case is the single move to strengthen CFO approval at 1 Jul meeting.

recommended_moves: Present AI iSaaS integration proposal directly to the CIO to secure IT resource commitment before 1 Jul CFO meeting.
Build and present quantified business case (ROI, cycle time reduction, or cost savings) to strengthen CFO approval at 1 Jul meeting.
Secure executive attendance at 1 Jul Vienna CFO meeting to match CFO peer level and signal strategic importance.

sources: 25 Jun 2026 call (Pölki): 'the CFO' referenced as decision authority; buyer requested 'management meeting to align on pricing/flexibility' for 1 Jul
24 Jun 2026 call (Pölki): 'without CFO/management override to commit IT resources, procurement preference alone is insufficient to close'
No CFO attendance on any recorded call (21 calls read)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Barbara Potisk-Eibensteiner is named in CRM MEDDPICC as Economic Buyer, and buyer confirmed 25 Jun 'the CFO' holds budget authority. However, the CFO has not appeared on any recorded call, has not committed budget/resources, and has not attended any Zycus engagement. Procurement champions (Pölki, Eppich, Koller) are building internal pressure but cannot override IT resistance without CFO intervention. Buyer requested management meeting 25 Jun for 1 Jul to secure CFO attendance and align on pricing/flexibility. If the CFO does not attend or cannot override IT resource gate, deal is structurally stalled. The economic buyer is identified but not engaged.
